question: [File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user send a base32 string containing only padding characters ('=') to getByAliasAndType(), causing BASE32.decode() to return an empty or null byte array that triggers a NullPointerException in findByAlias(), which is not caught by CustomExceptionResolver and crashes the worker thread?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with evmAddress set to '0x' (just the prefix, no hex digits), causing Hex.decodeHex('') to return an empty byte array, then ByteBuffer.wrap(new byte[0]) followed by buffer.getInt() to throw BufferUnderflowException that crashes the handler thread?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user send a high-rate stream of requests with varying evmAddress lengths (1-11 bytes decoded), each triggering a BufferUnderflowException, causing the JVM to spend excessive time on exception stack trace generation and garbage collection, degrading throughput to the point where transaction confirmation is impossible?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user send a base32 string that decodes to a byte array containing SQL wildcard characters or binary sequences that, when passed to the native query in findByAlias(), cause a DB-level error or unexpected query plan that locks tables and prevents transaction record insertion?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request where evmAddress is a string of whitespace characters (e.g., '   '), causing Hex.decodeHex() to throw a DecoderException that is rethrown as IllegalArgumentException, and by flooding with such requests saturate the GraphQL thread pool and prevent legitimate transaction queries from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user send a crafted GraphQL query that calls the account resolver with entityId repeatedly in a tight loop (e.g., using GraphQL aliases to batch 1000 account queries in a single request), causing getByIdAndType() to be called 1000 times per request and exhausting DB connections?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user send a GraphQL query with 1000 aliased account fields each using a different alias value, causing getByAliasAndType() to be called 1000 times per request, each issuing a DB query, exhausting the connection pool and preventing transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user send a GraphQL query with 1000 aliased account fields each using a different evmAddress, causing getByEvmAddressAndType() to be called 1000 times per request, each issuing a DB query, exhausting the connection pool and halting transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user send a deeply nested GraphQL query that triggers getByEvmAddressAndType() at multiple levels, causing exponential DB query amplification that exhausts the connection pool and prevents the mirror node from recording new transactions?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user send a GraphQL introspection query combined with a large number of account queries in a single request, causing the server to spend excessive CPU time on both introspection and DB lookups, starving the transaction processing pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user send a base32 string that is exactly at the maximum allowed length but contains only valid characters, causing decodeBase32() to allocate a large byte array and findByAlias() to pass it to the DB, and by sending thousands of such requests simultaneously exhaust both heap memory and DB connections?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with evmAddress containing only the '0x' prefix followed by a single hex character (e.g., '0xA'), causing Hex.decodeHex() to throw a DecoderException due to odd-length input, and by flooding with such requests overwhelm the exception handling path?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user send a valid 20-byte evmAddress where bytes 0-3 are non-zero (e.g., '0x0100000000000000000000000000000000000001'), causing the code to skip the findById path and call findByEvmAddress() with a 20-byte array, and by flooding with unique addresses exhaust DB connections and prevent transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user send a crafted EntityId where the encoded long ID collides with a system-critical entity (e.g., entity 0.0.2 which is the fee collection account), causing repeated DB lookups for that entity to create lock contention that delays transaction fee processing and halts confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user send a base32 string that decodes to a byte array of exactly 1 byte, causing findByAlias() to execute a SQL query with a 1-byte parameter that may match no rows but still consumes a DB connection, and by flooding with such requests exhaust the connection pool?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with evmAddress set to a string of 40 zeros ('0x' + '0'*40), causing the ByteBuffer check (getInt()==0 && getLong()==0) to be true and entityRepository.findById(0L) to be called, and by flooding with such requests exhaust DB connections?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with evmAddress that decodes to exactly 12 bytes where the first 4 bytes are zero and next 8 bytes are zero, causing entityRepository.findById(0L) to be called (reading the last 8 bytes as 0), and by flooding with such requests exhaust DB connections and prevent transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user send a crafted EntityId with shard=Long.MAX_VALUE, realm=Long.MAX_VALUE, num=Long.MAX_VALUE to getByIdAndType(), causing EntityId.of() to overflow and produce an unexpected entity ID that triggers a DB error, and by flooding with such requests crash the DB connection pool?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getByEvmAddressAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with evmAddress that decodes to exactly 20 bytes where the first 12 bytes are all zeros, causing entityRepository.findById() to be called with the last 8 bytes interpreted as a long, and if those 8 bytes represent Long.MIN_VALUE, trigger unexpected DB behavior that crashes the connection?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getByIdAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user send a high volume of concurrent GraphQL requests each containing multiple account queries with different entityId values, causing the Spring WebFlux/Servlet thread pool to be fully occupied with DB I/O waits, preventing the mirror node from ingesting new transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: graphql/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/graphql/service/EntityService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: getByAliasAndType()] Can an unprivileged external user send a base32 string that is millions of characters long to getByAliasAndType(), causing decodeBase32() to allocate a byte array of hundreds of kilobytes, and by sending many such requests simultaneously trigger a JVM OutOfMemoryError that kills the GraphQL service process?
